name: Chakravartin

visual_attributes.morphology: Regal human figure with divine attributes
distinctive_features: Imperial Gesture with clenched left hand at chest and right hand reaching up
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Surrounded by seven attributes including Chakraratna wheel, state elephant, charger horse, octagonal gem, empress, defense minister, and finance minister

domain_specific_visual.Saptaratna: Seven Jewels including Ratna-Chakra, Empress, Divine Jewellery, Immense Wealth, Huge Army of War-Chariots, Huge Army of Cavalry, Huge Army of Elephants

domain_specific_functional.Role_in_Jainism: World Conqueror with 32 major signs of excellence

relationships.description: Bharata was conferred the title of Chakravartin Samrāj
Ashoka is considered a historical Chakravartin

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Symbolizes ideal universal rulership and dharma
primary_sources: Upinder Singh's A History of Ancient and Early Medieval India
Dictionary of Hindu Lore and Legend by Anna Dallapiccola

contrastive_constraints: NOT Limited to a Single Religion
